paper.title: A high-resolution spectroscopic study of two new Na- and Al-rich field giants -- likely globular cluster escapees in the Galactic halo

review.summary: The paper presents high-resolution spectroscopic abundances for two metal-poor halo giants (SDSS J064655.6+411620.5 and SDSS J193712.01+502455.5) identified as likely globular cluster escapees based on Na/Al enrichment and C/O/Mg depletion patterns. Three internal LaTeX tables are present: one observation log (Table 1) with target coordinates, exposure details, SNR, radial velocities, and stellar parameters; and two abundance tables (Tables 2 and 3) listing elemental abundances for each program star. One external resource is cited: an online tool for C abundance evolutionary corrections (http://vplacco.pythonanywhere.com/). No machine-readable data files are distributed with the arXiv source. Comparison data from the SAGA database and Carretta et al. GC surveys are referenced but no concrete download URLs are provided.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents observational details and derived stellar atmospheric parameters for the two program stars (SDSS J064655.6+411620.5 and SDSS J193712.01+502455.5). Referenced in Section 2 as Table 1.

source_refs.caption: Observational Details for our Program Stars

columns.name: Object
columns.meaning: SDSS object designation for the program star

columns.name: RA
columns.meaning: Right Ascension (J2000) in sexagesimal format (HH MM SS.s)

columns.name: DEC
columns.meaning: Declination (J2000) in sexagesimal format (±DD MM SS.s)

columns.name: Exposure (secs)
columns.meaning: Total exposure time in seconds

columns.name: Frames
columns.meaning: Number of co-added spectroscopic frames

columns.name: SNR
columns.meaning: Signal-to-noise ratio of the combined spectrum

columns.name: V (mag)
columns.meaning: Apparent V-band magnitude

columns.name: Radial Vel. (km s^-1)
columns.meaning: Heliocentric radial velocity measured via cross-correlation with a synthetic template spectrum
columns.source_of_definition: table header; Section 2 text describes RV measurement method

columns.name: T_eff (K)
columns.meaning: Adopted effective temperature derived from photometric calibrations, excitation balance of Fe I lines, and H-alpha profile fitting
columns.source_of_definition: table header; Section 2 describes Teff determination methods

columns.name: log(g) (cgs)
columns.meaning: Adopted surface gravity derived from ionization balance of Fe I/Fe II, Gaia parallaxes, and Mg I line wing fitting
columns.source_of_definition: table header; Section 2 describes log(g) determination methods

columns.name: ξ
columns.meaning: Microturbulent velocity
columns.source_of_definition: table header; Section 2 mentions microturbulent velocity as a derived stellar parameter

columns.name: [Fe/H]
columns.meaning: Iron abundance relative to solar, [Fe/H] = log(N_Fe/N_H) - log(N_Fe/N_H)⊙

internal_tables.evidence: Table constructed from \begin{table}...\end{table} at lines 538-552 of gce.tex. Caption: 'Observational Details for our Program Stars'. Referenced in Section 2: 'Details of the observations, along with the signal-to-noise ratios and V magnitudes, for these two stars are listed in Table 1.' and 'The adopted values of the stellar parameters for both stars are shown in Table 1.'
internal_tables.comments: Single table with two data rows (one per program star). LaTeX format uses tabular with 12 columns separated by vertical bars.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents measured elemental abundances for the first program star SDSS J064655.6+411620.5. Referenced in Section 3 as Table 2: 'The complete abundance tables for SDSS J0646+4116 and SDSS J1937+5024 are provided in Tables 2 and 3 respectively.'

columns.name: Name
columns.meaning: Element name (e.g., Li, C, O, Na, Mg, Al, Ca, Sc, Ti, Cr, Mn, Co, Ni, Zn, Sr, Ba)

columns.name: Species
columns.meaning: Ionization state used for abundance determination (I = neutral, II = singly ionized; CH and CN indicate molecular band measurements)

columns.name: Solar
columns.meaning: Solar photospheric abundance A⊙(X) = log ε⊙(X) adopted from Asplund et al. (2009)
columns.source_of_definition: table header; Section 2 states 'Solar abundances for the individual elements are taken from Asplund et al. (2009)'

columns.name: lines
columns.meaning: Number of spectral absorption lines used for the abundance measurement

columns.name: A(X)
columns.meaning: Absolute elemental abundance on the scale log ε(X) = log(N_X/N_H) + 12

columns.name: [X/H]
columns.meaning: Element abundance relative to solar: [X/H] = A(X) - A⊙(X)

columns.name: [X/Fe]
columns.meaning: Element abundance relative to iron: [X/Fe] = [X/H] - [Fe/H]

columns.name: σ*
columns.meaning: Standard deviation (1-σ uncertainty) of the abundance measurement

internal_tables.evidence: Table constructed from \begin{table}...\end{table} at lines 554-583 of gce.tex. Caption: 'Elemental Abundances for SDSS J064655.6+411620.5'. Referenced at line 384: 'The complete abundance tables for SDSS J0646+4116 and SDSS J1937+5024 are provided in Tables 2 and 3 respectively.' Columns span 8 fields per row. Some elements have two rows (Ti, Cr) showing both neutral and singly-ionized measurements.
internal_tables.comments: Li and C (via CH band) have no [X/H] or [X/Fe] values shown (indicated by ...). O has only an upper limit. Abundances incorporate NLTE corrections where available.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents measured elemental abundances for the second program star SDSS J193712.01+502455.5. Referenced in Section 3 as Table 3: 'The complete abundance tables for SDSS J0646+4116 and SDSS J1937+5024 are provided in Tables 2 and 3 respectively.'

internal_tables.evidence: Table constructed from \begin{table}...\end{table} at lines 585-616 of gce.tex. Caption: 'Elemental Abundances for SDSS J193712.01+502455.5'. Referenced at line 384: 'The complete abundance tables for SDSS J0646+4116 and SDSS J1937+5024 are provided in Tables 2 and 3 respectively.' Columns span 8 fields per row. Some elements have two rows (Ti, Cr) showing both neutral and singly-ionized measurements.
internal_tables.comments: N abundance is an upper limit ([N/Fe] < +0.42). Li and C (via CH band) have no [X/H] or [X/Fe] values shown. Abundances incorporate NLTE corrections where available.

external_resources.description: Online tool for computing carbon abundance corrections associated with the evolutionary state of a star (the Placco et al. 2014 CEMP correction). Used to correct the measured C abundances for both program stars: corrections of +0.01 dex and +0.50 dex were applied to SDSS J0646+4116 and SDSS J1937+5024, respectively.

source_refs.context: Corrections to the C abundances associated with the evolutionary state for each star (Placco et al. 2014) have also been computed (http://vplacco.pythonanywhere.com/), and found to be +0.01 dex and +0.50 dex for J0646+4116 and J1937+5024, respectively.

external_resources.evidence: URL appears as a LaTeX footnote at line 319 of gce.tex: \footnote{http://vplacco.pythonanywhere.com/}. The surrounding text states the corrections were computed using the Placco et al. (2014) method.
external_resources.comments: This is the only concrete external data-asset URL given in the paper. No other machine-readable data files, catalog downloads, or data-availability URLs are provided.
